EF-0 TORNADO, WITH SPEEDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH, TOUCHED DOWN ON REDHEAD HILL ROAD IN NORTHERN WOODSTOCK AND TRAVELED 5 MILES ON A DISCONTINOUS PATH BEFORE LIFTING UP IN THE QUINEBAUG PORTION OF THOMPSON. IT WAS ONLY 8 YARDS WIDE. IT SLICED A SINGLE, HEALTHY OAK TREE IN HALF AND FLATTENED A SMALL PORTION OF TWO SEPARATE CORN FIELDS ON READHEAD HILL ROAD IN WOODSTOCK. ISOLATED TREE DAMAGE WAS ALSO SPOTTED ON WATSON ROAD, NEAR GREEN ACRES LANE IN QUINEBAUG. THE TORNADO LASTED FROM 936 AM TO 940 AM.
REPORTS OF TREES DOWN ALONG ROUTE 169 AND ROUTE 198.

TREES AND WIRES DOWN ON ROUTE 198 BLOCKING ROAD NEAR STURBRIDGE LINE. RED HEAD HILL ROAD HAS TREES DOWN.

*** 1 INJ *** A HIGH END EF-1 TORNADO, 110 MPH, STRUCK DUDLEY AND WEBSTER, WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE IN WEBSTER. IT WAS 300 YARDS WIDE, 0.5 MILES IN LENGTH, AND LASTED FROM 948 TO 949 AM. THERE WAS ONE MINOR INJURY. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTERN DUDLEY FROM ROUGHLY ROUTE 12 WEST OF LAPRISE COURT NORTHEASTWARD ONTO MAIN ST. IN WEBSTER AND LIFTED NEAR THE FRENCH RIVER BY OXFORD AVE. MANY TREES WERE SNAPPED/DEBARKED. THERE WAS STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO ROOFS, A GAS STATION OVERHANG, APARTMENTS AND BUSINESSES.

SCOFIELD AVENUE AND NEW BOSTON ROAD...ROAD BLOCKAGE DEBRIS IN ROAD. SEVERAL GRANITE SLABS WERE LIFTED AND THROWN IN THE ROADWAY. TREE DOWN ON WEST MAIN STREET AND CENTRAL AVENUE. ON MCQUAID LANE AT LAPRISE COURT...TREE AND WIRES DOWN LEANING ON HOUSE.

EF0 TORNADO WITH PATH LENGTH 0.4 MI, WIDTH 30 YDS, MAX WIND SPEED 70 MPH. TREES DOWNED W OF STORRS RD (RTE 195) BETWEEN CEMETERY & BASSETTS BRIDGE RD & E-NE ACROSS CEMETERY RD & ENDING JUST E OF SOUTHERNMOST ECHO RD. SEVERAL SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF A ROOF ON STORRS RD & FOUND 500 YDS DOWNSTREAM. A PERSON LIVING ON CENTRE ST OBSERVED A SWIRLING MASS OF LEAVES & STICKS TO HIS SE. THE MANSFIELD EM DIR FOUND DOWNED TREES N OF CEMETERY RD FACING SW WHILE TREES WERE FACING NE SOUTH OF CEMETERY RD.

TREES DOWNED WEST OF STORRS RD (ROUTE 195) BETWEEN CEMETERY RD AND BASSETTS BRIDGE RD AND CONTINUING EAST-NORTHEASTWARD ACROSS CEMETERY RD AND ENDING JUST TO THE EAST OF THE SOUTHERNMOST PORTION OF ECHO RD. SEVERAL SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF A ROOF ON STORRS RD AND FOUND 500 YDS DOWNSTREAM. A PERSON LIVING ON CENTRE ST, JUST WEST OF ECHO LAKE OBSERVED A SWIRLING MASS OF LEAVES AND STICKS PASSING TO HIS SOUTHEAST, SOME OF WHICH WERE HITTING HIS HOUSE. TREES DOWN ON GEORGE AND MAIN STREETS. POLE DOWN ON HASKELL STREET

A TORNADO AFFECTED NORTH PROVIDENCE (EF0 DAMAGE) AND LINCOLN (EF1 DAMAGE) FROM 331-334 PM EDT. THE TORNADO BEGAN ON MEADOW VIEW BLVD. IN NORTH PROVIDENCE WITH WIND SPEEDS UP TO 70 MPH. THE MAJORITY OF THE DAMAGE WAS IN LINCOLN ON ANGELL ROAD AND RIATA DRIVE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED AND SHINGLES WERE BLOWN OFF SEVERAL HOUSES. HIGHEST WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90-100 MPH IN LINCOLN. PATH LENGTH 1.5 MILES. PATH WIDTH 250 YARDS.

AN EF-1 TORNADO (90-95 MPH) TOUCHED DOWN FROM 403-406PM EDT ON OCT 23, 2018 FROM THE MIDDLE OF PATRILL HOLLOW RD IN HARDWICK, INTO A WOODED AREA NORTHWEST OF THE INTERSECTION OF PATRILL HOLLOW RD AND CZESKY RD. THE TORNADO'S PATH WAS 0.7 MILES LONG AND ABOUT 350 YARDS WIDE. MORE THAN 150 TREES, MANY OF WHICH WERE MEDIUM TO LARGE OAKS, WERE EITHER UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ON PATRILL HOLLOW RD. WHILE MOST WERE BLOWN DOWN FROM SW TO SE, WE FOUND SEVERAL BLOWN DOWN FROM E TO W, INDICATING ROTATION.
TREES DOWN WITH ROOF RIPPED OFF HOME IN THE AREA OF ANGELL ROAD
TREES DOWN WITH ROOF RIPPED OFF HOME IN THE AREA OF ANGEL ROAD

AN EF-1 TORNADO HIT A SMALL PORTION OF NORTON FROM 413-415 PM EDT. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 90-95 MPH. THE TORNADO BEGAN ON FREEMAN ST., JUST NORTH OF RTE 123. IT MOVED NORTHEAST, CUTTING ACROSS THE SOUTHERN PORTION OF NORTON RESERVOIR, ACROSS RTE 140, AND LIFTED ON THE SOUTHERNMOST PORTION OF RESERVOIR ST. NUMEROUS OAK AND PINE TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. A GARAGE SUFFERED DAMAGE FROM A FALLEN TREE. THERE WAS MINOR DAMAGE TO ONE HOME. PATH LENGTH 0.8 MILES. PATH WIDTH 170 YARDS.
TREE DOWN ON CAR ON TANGLEWOOD LANE

AN EF-0 TORNADO (70-80 MPH) TOUCHED DOWN FROM THE NORTHERN PART OF BRIGHAM POND IN HUBBARDSTON, TO JUST WEST OF THE SOUTHERN PORTION OF OLD WESTMINSTER ROAD, FROM 426-429 PM EDT ON OCTOBER 23, 2018. THE TORNADO'S PATH WAS ABOUT A MILE LONG, AND THE PATH WIDTH WAS ABOUT 100 YARDS. DOZENS OF TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED IN HALF. A FEW TELEPHONE POLES WERE SNAPPED AT THE TOP. SOME SIDING FROM A BUILDING WAS DISCOVERED ON OLD WESTMINSTER ROAD.

A WATERSPOUT IN VINEYARD SOUND MOVED ONSHORE IN WOODS HOLE, MA AND THEN QUICKLY DISSIPATED. AN EYEWITNESS AT THE WOODS HOLE GOLF CLUB SAW FOUR WOODEN CHAIRS LOFTED AND SWIRLING IN THE AIR AND THEY ENDED UP 500 FEET AWAY. IT WAS AN EF-0, WITH WINDS OF 60-65 MPH, A 0.1 MILE PATH LENGTH, AND A 10 YEAR PATH WIDTH.
